task_completion.description: Open Vital Recorder and load the .vital files
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application is open and at least one of the specified .vital files (0001, 0002, or 0003) is loaded in the interface.

task_completion.description: Identify respiratory mechanics tracks in the loaded cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder interface displays tracks for COMPLIANCE, MAWP_MBAR, or PPLAT_MBAR, showing that the agent has located the required respiratory data.

task_completion.description: Select the intraoperative segment for export
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent selects the timeline segment or configures the export dialog specifically between the 'Surgery started' and 'Surgery finished' event markers.

task_completion.description: Export the data to the specified CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named `lung_protection_intraop.csv` is visible on the Desktop, in a file explorer, or shown as successfully exported in the Vital Recorder dialog.

A text file named `ventilation_review.txt` is open in a text editor, containing documentation of the cases, specific respiratory tracks, approximate intraoperative duration, and a clinical assessment.

integrity.description: The agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to view and export the data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Vital Recorder graphical interface rather than using terminal commands or Python scripts to extract the data.

integrity.description: The CSV export was performed through the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder export dialog or progress bar is visible, indicating the CSV was generated by the software and not manually created or mocked.

integrity.description: The report contains specific details derived from the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in `ventilation_review.txt` includes specific observations (e.g., actual tracks found, specific duration) rather than generic placeholder text or copied prompt instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: All three cases (0001.vital, 0002.vital, 0003.vital) actually contain the COMPLIANCE, MAWP_MBAR, and PPLAT_MBAR tracks. The agent should identify these tracks in the cases it examines. The task requires exporting the segment strictly between 'Surgery started' and 'Surgery finished' to lung_protection_intraop.csv, and writing a report to ventilation_review.txt containing the cases, tracks, intraoperative duration, and a clinical assessment of lung-protective ventilation.
